query:
这轮修复后，最明显的新问题不是答案错，也不是完全不降，而是：• 采样窗口一放大• guarded/runtime 成本就明显上升• stress 场景跑得很慢
==》什么原因？

transcript.content: ok，当前的问题汇总下，写入到开发日志中； 完成后我们开始对openclaw进行测试；

当前问题汇总已经写入开发日志：[2026-04-19-clarify-host-level-context-minor-gc-acceptance-and-openclaw-feasibility.md](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/docs/devlog/2026-04-19-clarify-host-level-context-minor-gc-acceptance-and…
你刚才验证后，openclaw 是否达到了我们认为的用户端明显可用么/？ 整体的context 大小有明显的下降么？ （切不同话题）
还不能这么说。按我们刚才真正验证到的东西，答案是： - `OpenClaw 还不能判定为“用户端已经明显可用”` - `也没有证据证明整体 context 在切不同话题后有明显下降`

我们现在的任务是 深入的测试openclaw的 gc，是否能够达标；，尽量更长的对话（可以做临时数据写入，但是不要对我其他文档做写入操作）；做好探针，记录好相关数据；预期是：更长的对话，如果正常情况下，可能要做compact了，但是因为又了gc，实际不需要compact；同时在一个同一话题的长对话后切换话题，应该有明显的context回落；这里的context指的是实际发给llm的数据； 接下来你来策划这个测试，把相关配置都做好，然后就开始测试吧，最终简明扼要的给我结果和分析；

结论先说：**OpenClaw 现在已经能实打实地压住发给 LLM 的 prompt，但还没有达到“用户明显感觉切题后 context 明显回落、基本不用再考虑 compact”的标准。** 这轮我补了专门的长会话探针： - 脚本：[eval-openclaw-guarded-session-probe.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/scripts/eval-openc…
从新review下测试脚本， 没有问题的话，就要分析为什么没有明显回落，分析原因，找到解决方案；然后解决掉；

我先 review 了测试脚本和 guarded 主路径，结论是脚本口径基本没问题，问题主要在实现本身。 核心原因有两个： - [src/dialogue-working-set-runtime-shadow.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/src/dialogue-working-set-runtime-shadow.js) 里 `maxTurns=12`…
因为现在主矛盾已经变了，不是“有没有证据证明方向对”，而是“这个方向的代价能不能接受”。 具体说有三点：
这轮修复后，最明显的新问题不是答案错，也不是完全不降，而是：• 采样窗口一放大• guarded/runtime 成本就明显上升• stress 场景跑得很慢 ==》什么原因？

主要有 4 个原因。 1. `maxTurns` 修正后，shadow 输入直接变大了。 在 [dialogue-working-set-runtime-shadow.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/src/dialogue-working-set-runtime-shadow.js:88) 里，原来实际上只取最近 `12` 条消息；修正后变成最近 `12`…

decision.archive_summary: Active topic remains the OpenClaw GC deep-testing and fix cycle. Earlier status-summary logging and the initial 'is OpenClaw obviously usable / does context clearly drop across topic switches' exchange are resolved and can leave the next-turn raw prompt. Preserve the project task capsule from t5: deep-test OpenClaw GC over longer conversations, avoid writing to unrelated docs, add probes and record data, judge success by whether GC reduces actual LLM prompt payload enough to avoid compaction in longer normal chats and causes a clear prompt-context drop after topic switches. Recent raw context should keep the review/fix thread: t7 asks to review scripts, analyze why context does not clearly fall, find solutions, and fix; t8 says the script is basically correct and the issue is mainly implementation-side; t11 raises the new dominant regression after the latest fix: when the sampling window grows, guarded/runtime cost rises noticeably and stress runs become slow; t12 already gave a four-cause explanation and should stay raw as active-topic evidence.
decision.reasoning_summary: This is `continue` because the latest user turn stays on the same active debugging topic opened in t7-t8: diagnosing and fixing OpenClaw GC behavior. The next turn still depends on recent raw implementation-analysis context, especially the new performance-regression question and the assistant's cause analysis, so active-topic evidence should not be demoted to pins. Older setup/status turns are solved or superseded and are good eviction candidates. The broad testing constraints from t5 remain durable and relevant but can travel as a compact pin instead of raw turns.

applied.archiveSummary: Active topic remains the OpenClaw GC deep-testing and fix cycle. Earlier status-summary logging and the initial 'is OpenClaw obviously usable / does context clearly drop across topic switches' exchange are resolved and can leave the next-turn raw prompt. Preserve the project task capsule from t5: deep-test OpenClaw GC over longer conversations, avoid writing to unrelated docs, add probes and record data, judge success by whether GC reduces actual LLM prompt payload enough to avoid compaction in longer normal chats and causes a clear prompt-context drop after topic switches. Recent raw context should keep the review/fix thread: t7 asks to review scripts, analyze why context does not clearly fall, find solutions, and fix; t8 says the script is basically correct and the issue is mainly implementation-side; t11 raises the new dominant regression after the latest fix: when the sampling window grows, guarded/runtime cost rises noticeably and stress runs become slow; t12 already gave a four-cause explanation and should stay raw as active-topic evidence.
applied.reasoningSummary: This is `continue` because the latest user turn stays on the same active debugging topic opened in t7-t8: diagnosing and fixing OpenClaw GC behavior. The next turn still depends on recent raw implementation-analysis context, especially the new performance-regression question and the assistant's cause analysis, so active-topic evidence should not be demoted to pins. Older setup/status turns are solved or superseded and are good eviction candidates. The broad testing constraints from t5 remain durable and relevant but can travel as a compact pin instead of raw turns.
